summary: Closed M1-GATE after PR #24 merged into main. The M1 milestone exit gate is integrated at main merge commit cd19e1071d3264c5fd9a2d97bea35d677882d7ec; project state now advances to M2 under the existing autonomous-goal policy, with M5 still reserved as a Human Gate.

tests_run.result: PR #24 MERGED at 2026-06-24T10:53:39Z with merge commit cd19e1071d3264c5fd9a2d97bea35d677882d7ec; Quality/Web and Windows Desktop Smoke checks both succeeded.
M1-GATE merge commit is an ancestor of origin/main.
M1-GATE is CLOSED; SIM-001 through SIM-006 are CLOSED.
No dependency-ready task printed after M1-GATE closure; M2 task graph creation is the next orchestration step.

decisions: Close M1-GATE only after confirming the accepted gate entered origin/main through PR #24.
Advance current_milestone from M1 to M2 in the post-merge closure commit.
Preserve M1 follow-up risks for Chromium/Electron host hash canary and sim-core/content-runtime boot-boundary contract tests.
Do not start M2 implementation in this closure commit.
risks: M2 task graph still needs to be created and independently reviewed before M2 implementation begins.
M1-GATE follow-ups remain active and should be scheduled into M2 validation/integration.
requested_action: After this closure commit is integrated, create or validate the M2 task graph before starting M2 implementation work.
